isMaterialIssue.description: CdoId: 4650; FieldId: 689966676; Workspace: 60; Flags: None; Annotations: This flag is used when ResourceComponentSetup or ComponentIssue is transacted. The transaction will not be allowed if there is a thawing requirement and the container has yet to reach the thawing duration. Likewise the transaction will fail if there is an exposure requirement and container has exceeded the exposure duration.

isMaterialReturn.description: CdoId: 4650; FieldId: 689966677; Workspace: 60; Flags: None; Annotations: This flag is used when a container Moves to a Spec. Note that the destination Spec's attribute is used for the logic. The container's Product's MaterialMaxReturns will be used to verify whether a move to such a Spec is allowed. Once the lot hits beyond the value (considered only if > 0), the container will not be allowed to move to the Spec. If not violated, the container's MaterialTotalExposure will be accumulated if its Product's MaterialAccumulateExposure is set to true and there is a MaterialWithdrawalDate value. Then, the container's MaterialTotalReturns will be incremented by 1, the MaterialWithdrawalDate nullified and the MaterialExposureOffset set to 0.

isMaterialWithdraw.description: CdoId: 4650; FieldId: 689966671; Workspace: 60; Flags: None; Annotations: This flag is used when a container Moves out of a Spec and is used to represent the start of the material's exposure duration. The container's MaterialWithdrawalDate will be set to the Move transaction date only if it is null. Otherwise the difference between the MaterialWithdrawalDate and the Move transaction date will be updated to the container's MaterialExposureOffset. This offset is used to represent a temporary stoppage of the exposure.

maxQueueTime.description:
CdoId: 4650; FieldId: 3956; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This is not implemented in the current version of InSite.

This value determines the maximum length of time a Container can remain in an in-queue state at the Step that uses this Spec. The value for MaximumQueueTimeAction determines the action to take if the in-queue time exceeds this value.

maxQueueTimeAction.description:
CdoId: 4650; FieldId: 3957; Workspace: csi; Flags: None; Annotations: This is not implemented in the current version of InSite.

This value is used to determine the action to take when a Service (transaction) is started for a Container that has been in-queue longer than the allowable queue time (MaxQueueTime). 

The values for this option are:
0 = No action
1 = Warning to Operator
2 = Warning to Operator requiring a password to continue
3 = Place container on Hold
4 = Do not allow transaction to continue

These values and their associated actions are intended to match the values and actions for field validations.

This action applies to any service that changes a Container from an In-Queue to an In-Process state.

thruputRequirement.description:
CdoId: 4650; FieldId: 689966669; Workspace: 60; Flags: None; Annotations: Indicate the requirement for Thruput when a container is executing a move transaction. Valid values include:
i)	OPTIONAL - Thruput is not required.
ii)	AUTO - Thruput will be automatically triggered before move. Always full quantity thruput is done.
iii)	MANUALPARTIAL - Thruput is required but does not need to have the full container's quantity thruput. Error will be raised during move if no quantity thruput detected.
iv)	MANUALFULL - Thruput is required and the container's quantity must be fully thruput. Error will be raised during move if quantity thruput is not full.
The ProcessThruputRequirement service flag must be set to true. Currently MoveStd and Rework is having it set to true.
